methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Navier is a seed-stage transportation company that has raised $7.2M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of three private transportation firms (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride); no public comparables exist in a sufficiently narrow peer set.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method. The Stage-Progression approach applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $7M total-raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $18M. This reflects typical step-up dynamics from Seed to Series A for transportation ventures. A Peer-Median triangulation method is not applicable here: while three transportation peers exist sector-wide, none qualify as same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with sufficient data density to establish a reliable median. Using an all-sector median would introduce material noise given the heterogeneity of transportation sub-segments and company maturity levels.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M to $21M, centered on $18M. This narrow range reflects confidence in the stage-progression logic but also the constraint of a single-method foundation. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage peer benchmarks to validate the 2.5x multiplier in this specific sector, and limited visibility into Navier's technical progress and go-to-market traction relative to the named comparables. A Series A round would provide pricing discovery; until then, this range represents the upper bound of supportable valuation given available data.
